name: Brocéliande Forest
primary_sources: Le Chevalier au lion by Chrétien de Troyes

visual_attributes.morphology: vast, dense forest with mystical and ancient appearance
visual_attributes.texture: lush and verdant with an ethereal glow
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: interwoven trees and clearings with mystical landmarks

domain_specific_visual.Arthurian_elements: presence of mythical and magical features

functional_attributes.primary_function: setting for magical and mythical events
secondary_functions: site of legendary battles and events

domain_specific_functional.Arthurian_role: central to stories involving Merlin and Morgan le Fay

relationships.description: Merlin is said to have been imprisoned or died here.
Morgan's Vale of No Return is located here.
Site of various adventures and battles involving knights.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: mythically located in Brittany, often identified with Paimpont forest
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: central to Arthurian legends and medieval European imagination
primary_sources: Le Chevalier au lion by Chrétien de Troyes

contrastive_constraints: NOT a modern or urban setting
